headline: 10 July 2026
subtitle: IRGC Phase 2 strikes Jordan; Hormuz shipping at effective zero; Brent eases to $76.37

metrics.metric: Days since last confirmed cyber incident (Gulf FS)

items.bold_heading: No new Kuwait alerts in this window
items.text: No new Kuwait-specific air raid alerts confirmed after 07:00 UTC+3 on 9 July. The overnight siren activation (03:40 UTC+3 on 9 July — 3 ballistic + 1 cruise + 10 drones intercepted; 1 person injured) was covered in Report #103 and falls within the prior digest window. Civil Defence's standing precautionary advisory remains in force; public directed to follow MOD/MOI instructions, avoid sharing site imagery, and remain alert.

items.bold_heading: Power fully restored; no new infrastructure damage
items.text: Kuwait General Staff confirmed all overnight threats intercepted. Ministry of Electricity confirmed supply restored to all residential areas affected by shrapnel damage to 7 overhead transmission lines. No new infrastructure damage confirmed in this window.

items.bold_heading: Jordan — Al-Azraq Air Base (9 July, afternoon UTC+3)
items.text: IRGC Phase 2 retaliation — 10 Iranian ballistic missiles fired at US forces at Al-Azraq Air Base, Jordan. Jordan Armed Forces confirmed 8 missiles intercepted; interception debris fell in uninhabited areas; no casualties. IRGC claimed destruction of 'command and control centre in West Asia.' Jordan stated it will not allow its airspace to be used by either side.

items.bold_heading: CENTCOM — third consecutive day of strikes on Iran (9 July)
items.text: US forces struck additional Iranian military targets including Bushehr IRGC facility, Chabahar port, and Iranshahr airport. Gulf News additionally reports fresh strikes near Asaluyeh (South Pars/North Dome LNG complex) overnight 9–10 July; US did not publicly confirm at compilation time. Cumulative CENTCOM strikes over 3 days: approximately 170 targets.

items.bold_heading: Strait of Hormuz — effective cargo shipping halt (7–10 July, ongoing)
items.text: Lloyd's List Intelligence: no large vessel (>10,000 dwt) transited via the Southern Highway with AIS on since 7 July. Only approximately 5 vessels tracked crossing the strait on 8 July versus ~130/day pre-war. UK MTOO characterised posture as 'cautious' amid 'elevated threat environment.' Qatar paused South Pars LNG production ramp-up.

items.bold_heading: KWI Airport — continued disruption (ongoing)
items.text: No new physical attack on airport infrastructure. Terminal 1 under repair from 3 June strike; Terminals 4/5 operational. EASA CZIB (8 July): Kuwait FIR — no overflights; OKKK only with prior approval until 31 August 2026. International schedule disruptions consistent with prior 24h.

items.bold_heading: Power grid fully restored
items.text: Ministry of Electricity confirmed supply restored to all affected areas. Ministry Undersecretary Adel Al-Zamel confirmed completion of repairs on all 7 damaged overhead transmission lines. Critical facilities on backup should resume normal grid connection verification protocols.

items.bold_heading: Airport (KWI): Partial; EASA restrictions in force
items.text: Terminal 1 under repair; Kuwait Airways and Jazeera Airways operating; international carriers on reduced schedules. EASA CZIB (Kuwait FIR: no overflights, OKKK only with prior approval) in force until 31 August 2026. Short-notice closure risk elevated.

items.bold_heading: UAE President solidarity visit to Kuwait Amir
items.text: UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al-Nahyan made official visit to Kuwait, meeting Amir Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah. Both leaders reaffirmed strategic partnership — visible GCC solidarity signal amid active conflict.

items.bold_heading: Ports: ISPS Level 2; Borders: Saudi open, Iraq closed
items.text: Shuaiba and Shuwaikh ports at ISPS Level 2; commercial operations continuing, no new incidents. Saudi crossing (Salmy/Nuwaisib) open with delays. Iraq border (Safwan/Abdali) restricted/closed — land evacuation only via Saudi Arabia.
sources: Lloyd's List Middle East Port Update — ongoing

items.bold_heading: Essential services normal; civil defence guidance in effect
items.text: MEW confirms water and power supply normal across all districts following repair completion. Schools in summer recess. Standing civil defence guidance remains in effect for the public.
sources: MEW (Ministry of Electricity and Water) — 9 July 2026

items.text: CENTCOM third-day strikes (~170 cumulative targets over Jul 7–9); Iran Phase 2 retaliation expanded to Jordan (10 ballistic missiles, Al-Azraq). Conflict geographic footprint now spans from Strait of Hormuz to Levant, with Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, Jordan, and Iraq all drawn into the exchange.

Diplomacy alive but fragile: US official confirmed technical nuclear talks continue; Pakistan and Qatar working to restore ceasefire channel. Mojtaba Khamenei still has not made a public appearance as of 10 July — leadership uncertainty keeps IRGC in maximum-autonomy mode; his first public statement is the most consequential Iranian signal of the crisis.

Strait of Hormuz shipping at effective zero since 7 July. Goldman Sachs warned disruptions will set back regional crude recovery; TD Securities projects Brent +$10–15 over summer as inventories dwindle. Qatar's South Pars LNG ramp-up pause adds a supply-side shock.

items.bold_heading: Staff safety and BCP — maintain elevated readiness
items.text: No new Kuwait attack in window; power restored. IRGC Phase 2 Jordan targeting demonstrates geographic escalation. Reconfirm next-of-kin lists and 24h contact protocols for Kuwait-based staff; maintain WFH/remote capacity; note Iraq border closed — land evacuation only via Saudi Arabia.

items.bold_heading: Power grid — post-repair verification required
items.text: All 7 lines restored, but the Jul 7–9 sequence proved interception shrapnel can take down multiple grid segments simultaneously. Run post-restoration audit: confirm UPS/generator integrity at all critical sites; re-verify cooling continuity for data centres that may have switched to generator during the outage window.

items.bold_heading: Payment/SWIFT continuity — widened to Eastern Med
items.text: Jordan's inclusion in IRGC Phase 2 strikes adds Eastern Mediterranean payment corridors to the risk map. Monitor SWIFT BIC connectivity for all GCC and Levant correspondents; validate alternate clearing pathways for time-sensitive settlements.

items.bold_heading: Oil price — temporary pullback, summer upside risk
items.text: Brent eased to $76.37 from $78.02 (Jul 8 close) as markets priced in partial diplomatic signals. TD Securities and Goldman Sachs both forecast upside pressure as summer inventories tighten. Review energy loan book mark-to-market; reassess oil-linked margin call positions on revised price curve.

items.bold_heading: Sanctions exposure — Iran oil rush via dark fleet
items.text: Bloomberg reports Iran rushed ~11M barrels of crude exports in 24–48h before potential US blockade reimposition. Increased dark-fleet transactions through Gulf SWIFT nodes elevate sanctions-evasion risk. Validate commodity trade finance counterparty screening against updated OFAC SDN lists.

items.bold_heading: War Risk Premia — 8–10× baseline; July 10 repricing expected
items.text: Lloyd's market WRP for Hormuz transit now 8–10× pre-war baseline; per-voyage additional cost for a large vessel exceeds $400K–$600K. Following Jul 7–8 tanker attacks and subsequent shipping halt, underwriters will reprice on 10–11 July. Institutions with trade finance or commodity finance backed by Gulf-transit cargoes must confirm updated cover from brokers before next business day.

items.bold_heading: JWC Listed Areas — entire Arabian Gulf; no downgrade path
items.text: Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, Oman remain JWC Listed Areas since 3 March. Shipping halt and renewed kinetic activity make Q4 2026 downgrade scenario implausible. Some H&M underwriters are issuing mid-term endorsements restricting Hormuz transit cover. Confirm war risk riders on all Gulf-transit trade finance collateral are in force and cover current Listed Area scope.

items.bold_heading: Qatar LNG ramp-up pause — cargo and receivables risk
items.text: Qatargas paused South Pars LNG production expansion amid security risk. Institutions with LNG-indexed structured products, commodity finance, or receivables backed by Qatari supply chains should trigger contract review for force majeure provisions. IMO reports ~6,000 seafarers stranded in the Persian Gulf.

articles.title: Renewed U.S.-Iran Strikes in Hormuz Threaten Energy Market Recovery
articles.author: Staff analysis
articles.summary: Covers the structural collapse of the interim ceasefire — both parties accusing each other of violations while continuing strikes — with attention to energy market asymmetric risk: diplomatic re-engagement caps upside, but full Hormuz closure is the much larger downside. Notes the deal had no enforcement mechanism.
articles.relevance: Directly maps to oil price and Hormuz disruption dynamics affecting energy loan book, trade finance collateral, and commodity hedging; the 'no enforcement mechanism' point reinforces August 21 MoU expiry as a hard-deadline planning trigger.

articles.title: Iran Embraces a Forever War
articles.author: Mohammad Ayatollahi Tabaar
articles.summary: Tabaar argues Iran has concluded the war advances its multi-polar power status, and that diplomacy is only acceptable on Iran's terms ($24B upfront, 5-year enrichment cap). Iran will sustain low-grade conflict indefinitely rather than accept a structurally disadvantageous peace.
articles.relevance: The forever-war framework is the strategic backdrop against which the August 21 MoU expiry must be calibrated — not as a finish line but as a potential reset point for an indefinite conflict posture. Note: published 2 June 2026; extended per fallback protocol.

items.bold_heading: DDoS/defacement retaliation wave — 24–72h window elevated
items.text: IRGC Phase 2 strikes (Jordan, 9 July) confirm kinetic escalation threshold met for a pro-Iran cyber proxy package. Established 2026 pattern: Handala, FAD Team, Cyber Islamic Resistance umbrella launch coordinated DDoS/defacement campaigns within 24–72h of multi-state kinetic events. Financial services is consistently the #2 target sector.
items.threat_actor: Handala, FAD Team, Cyber Islamic Resistance
items.recommended_action: Maintain enhanced SOC watch mode; pre-provision CDN/DDoS mitigation surge capacity; validate WAF rule updates; keep IR team on standby for activation before next business-day open.

items.bold_heading: OT/SCADA post-repair monitoring gap — elevated
items.text: Kuwait's 7 transmission lines restored, but emergency repair windows create periods of reduced OT monitoring coverage. CISA AA26-097a remains in force (Iranian APT targeting Rockwell/Allen-Bradley PLCs in energy and government sectors).
items.recommended_action: Post-repair, verify IT/OT segmentation integrity; confirm OT monitoring tools resumed telemetry; review any anomalous PLC access events during the emergency window (9 July 00:00–07:00 UTC+3).

items.bold_heading: Seedworm/MuddyWater — new tasking cycle expected
items.text: Iran's multi-state Phase 2 retaliation typically precedes a new Seedworm tasking wave within 48–72h; group remains active on financial sector and airport networks since February 2026. Gulf FIs with US correspondent banking relationships face lateral movement risk.
items.recommended_action: Run EDR query for LOLBin indicators on endpoints connecting to US correspondents; review recent Seedworm IOCs (updated post-July 7); alert threat intel team.

items.bold_heading: Mojtaba Khamenei absent = expanded IRGC cyber autonomy
items.text: New Supreme Leader has not made any public appearance since assuming office in March; IRGC commanders retain maximum delegated authority for offensive operations including cyber. Until Mojtaba asserts civilian oversight, the structural constraint on IRGC cyber escalation is absent.

items.bold_heading: Wiper capability — deployment threshold at lowest since conflict start
items.text: Three-day kinetic exchange with five-state targeting removes residual diplomatic restraint on Iranian destructive cyber payloads. Qatar's mediation role damaged; Pakistan back-channel not yet established; no active diplomatic brake on IRGC cyber escalation. Handala, Druidfly, FAD Team retain confirmed wiper capability (BibiWiper, Hatef).

items.bold_heading: Iran oil-export rush — elevated SWIFT sanctions-evasion exposure
items.text: Bloomberg reports Iran rushed ~11M barrels of crude exports in 24–48h before potential US blockade reimposition. Increased dark-fleet transactions through Gulf SWIFT nodes amplify both regulatory sanctions-violation risk and APT lateral movement risk for correspondent banks.
items.threat_actor: Iranian state APT (Charming Kitten / APT35 and affiliated)

items.text: Mojtaba Khamenei's first public appearance
items.why_it_matters: His emergence (or continued absence) is the most consequential Iranian signal in the conflict — appearance with IRGC commanders signals delegated war authority; civilian-format address signals negotiating pivot. Tasnim News Agency flagged a possible condolence ceremony this weekend.

items.text: Pakistan/Qatar diplomatic back-channel (48h window)
items.why_it_matters: CNN confirmed both countries working to restore ceasefire. If a new format is established within 24–48h a brief de-escalation window opens; if Qatar's role is further damaged by IRGC activity, the channel collapses and August 21 becomes a hard-cliff transition.

items.text: KWI airport — follow-up strike risk and NOTAM changes
items.why_it_matters: EASA CZIB in force; Terminal 1 still damaged from June 3; IRGC has demonstrated intent to target Kuwaiti aviation infrastructure. A follow-up strike on KWI or extension of no-fly zones triggers immediate airport suspension and emergency staff travel protocols.

source_confidence.note: No new Kuwait alert confirmed after 07:00 UTC+3 on 9 July; prior window's overnight event well-sourced via KUNA and Anadolu Agency
Jordan intercepts confirmed across RFERL, ANI, Jordan News, Times of Israel; CENTCOM strikes confirmed Gulf News/NBC; shipping halt confirmed Lloyd's List/Bloomberg/UK MTOO
Power restoration confirmed Kuwait Times, Arab News, Times Kuwait; UAE visit confirmed KUNA; border status consistent with prior reports
All major kinetic events confirmed; Mojtaba absence confirmed Al Jazeera; oil/LNG dynamics confirmed Bloomberg
Financial risk extrapolated from confirmed kinetic/market data; sanctions item per Bloomberg single source [SINGLE SOURCE]
WRP 8-10× estimate from Hormuz Strait Monitor and Bahrain Intelligence; July 10 underwriter repricing not yet published at compilation time
Pattern-based 24–72h projection; no confirmed new Gulf FS cyber incident in window; OT advisory is standing CISA guidance
Mojtaba/IRGC autonomy assessment is analytical; wiper threat confirmed capability but deployment unconfirmed; oil rush item per Bloomberg single source [SINGLE SOURCE]
